title: Jaltomata biflora
intro: Jaltomata biflora, also known as the two-flowered jaltomata, is a plant species in the family Solanaceae, commonly found in the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. Bioactive constituents of Jaltomata biflora include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. Jaltomata biflora is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are traditionally consumed to relieve respiratory symptoms and promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Jaltomata biflora is classified as a species within the genus Jaltomata, which is part of the family Solanaceae. This family is commonly known as the nightshade family, comprising over 2,800 species. Jaltomata biflora is further classified into the order Solanales and the kingdom Plantae. The classification places it within the larger clade of flowering plants, angiosperms, and the clade of dicotyledonous plants. The precise classification for Jaltomata biflora is Jaltomata biflora (R.E. Fr.)  Britton & Rose.
uses: Jaltomata biflora has been traditionally used in folk medicine in various parts of the world. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help in treating ailments such as fever and sore throats. Its leaves and stems are sometimes used to make tea, which is thought to have antioxidant and antibacterial effects. In some cultures, Jaltomata biflora is used to treat digestive issues and skin conditions. However, more research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using this plant for medicinal purposes.

These constituents are responsible for the plant's potential medicinal properties. Research has shown that Jaltomata biflora extracts exhibit antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities.
